title: Intensely Buttery Venezuelan Dough Flatbread
description: A flexitarian-friendly, intensely buttery flatbread from Venezuela featuring dill pickles, perfect as a post-workout snack, made with long-fermentation dough for a unique flavor and texture. It is shaggy and delicious, ideal for those looking for a savory treat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and roll it out into a large rectangle.
Brush the melted butter evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle chopped dill pickles, beaten egg, and black pepper over the butter.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling to form a crust.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes or until it is golden brown.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
